The prospect of Germany officially banning the AfD before July 1, 2025, is extremely low. Under German law, the only entity with the authority to ban a political party is the Federal Constitutional Court (Bundesverfassungsgericht). An application can only be submitted by the Federal Government, the Bundestag, or the Bundesrat. According to search results up to the May 2, 2025 knowledge cutoff, a motion to apply for a ban was debated by a group of parliamentarians in January 2025, but no application had yet been officially filed. 

Historically, party ban proceedings at the Federal Constitutional Court are extremely protracted. Previous cases (such as the two NPD ban proceedings) took years to resolve; for example, the second attempt was initiated around 2013/2014 and the final ruling rejecting it was in 2017. Even if an application had been filed in early 2025 (which it apparently has not), there is no expedited precedent or plausible pathway for the Court to conclude a complex trial on banning a major political party and issue an official ban before July 1, 2025. This makes the resolution 'Yes' nearly impossible.

The US State Department increased the travel advisory for Tanzania to Level 3 ("Reconsider Travel") on January 21, 2025, due to unrest, crime, terrorism, and specific targeting of marginalized groups. This triggers a Yes resolution, as the question simply asks if Tanzania will receive a Level 3 advisory before July 1, 2025. Numerous official US Embassy updates from early 2025 feature a "Travel Advisory Level 3" banner for Tanzania.
